rules.text: PAUL state file is at .paul/STATE.md. Read it before suggesting any work. Check loop position before touching code.
Loop order is PLAN → APPLY → UNIFY. Never suggest writing code outside /paul:apply. Never skip UNIFY after APPLY.
Phase plan files live at .paul/phases/{phase-id}/{plan-id}-PLAN.md. Never edit these manually — PAUL manages them.
Next approved action: /paul:apply .paul/phases/01-foundation/01-01-PLAN.md. Plan is finalized — do not re-plan unless user directs.
Competitor scraper code lives in apps/competitor-scraper/. All APPLY file operations target that subdirectory. Root .paul/ is the workspace PAUL home.

decisions.rationale: Forensic review found internal_sku and internal_price_history missing — fatal to Phase 5 reporting
Shaped architecture, rigor, and phase structure from project inception

decisions.decision: ExceptionQueue taxonomy defined as 6 named constants
decisions.rationale: MATCH_CONFIDENCE_LOW | PARSE_FAILED | PRICE_ANOMALY | JSON_INVALID | SITE_BLOCKED | SKU_REF_ERROR — prevents free-text exception types from becoming unmaintainable

decisions.decision: 5 intelligence metrics added to Phase 5 scope
decisions.rationale: OOS Rate, Loyalty Gap, Band Width, Promo Freq, New SKU Detection — all derivable from existing scrape data, no new tables needed

rules.text: Scraping scope: Woolworths NZ and New World NZ only. Auckland region. Free range eggs only. Never expand beyond this without explicit user approval.
Public data only. No login credentials, no CAPTCHA bypass, no anti-bot circumvention. If a page requires auth, stop and report — never attempt workarounds.
Primary scraper: @mendable/firecrawl-js. Fallback: Playwright (for JS-heavy pages + screenshot evidence). Never invert this order.
Every observation must persist source_url and raw_json_snapshot. These are the audit trail — never omit them.
screenshot_path must be relative only: "{run_id}/{filename}.png". Never store absolute paths. CHECK constraint in schema enforces this — trust it.
scrape_run_id must be crypto.randomUUID(). Never use sequential integers or timestamps as run IDs.
observed_at must be the actual HTTP response timestamp from the scraper. No DEFAULT in SQL — scraper must supply this value explicitly.
store_url values are intentionally null until Phase 2 research confirms them. Do not hardcode store URLs during Phase 1.

decisions.decision: Firecrawl as primary scraper, Playwright as fallback
decisions.rationale: Firecrawl handles JS rendering and returns structured JSON. Playwright needed for screenshot evidence and pages Firecrawl can't parse.

rules.text: Always PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON and journal_mode = WAL. These are non-negotiable for data integrity and performance.
Resolve DATABASE_PATH to absolute at startup: path.resolve(process.env.DATABASE_PATH ?? fallback). Never use cwd-relative paths at call time.
All migrations must be idempotent: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS. Running db:setup twice must produce zero errors.
better-sqlite3 is synchronous. Don't wrap DB calls in unnecessary Promise chains — lean into the sync API.
Schema has 8 tables in FK-dependency order: competitor → competitor_store → internal_sku → internal_price_history → competitor_product → scrape_run → competitor_price_observation → exception_queue.
Zod validates scrape output before any DB write. If Zod rejects, write to exception_queue — never write partial/unvalidated data to observation tables.

decisions.decision: SQLite for MVP, PostgreSQL deferred to production
decisions.rationale: Zero-config, single-file, sufficient for weekly manual operator runs. Adds complexity with no benefit until scale demands it.

decisions.decision: internal_price_history table added (was not in original spec)
decisions.rationale: Single current_price on internal_sku would lose history when operator updates pricing. Historical gap analysis in Phase 5 requires time-series price data.

rules.text: Phase 5 reporting requires operator to populate internal_sku.current_price before first report run. Document this dependency clearly — do not assume it's populated.
Exports: ExcelJS for .xlsx output, @react-pdf/renderer for PDF. Do not introduce alternative libraries.
Dashboard (Phase 6): Vite + vanilla JS. 3 screens only: dashboard, competitor prices, reports. No heavy framework — keep it lightweight.
All report generation must handle zero observations gracefully. Never throw on empty data — return empty-state output with a clear message.
Email distribution via Resend. Implement allowlist + dry-run flag before enabling (deferred to Plan 05-02).

decisions.decision: 5 intelligence metrics added to Phase 5 scope
decisions.rationale: OOS Rate, Loyalty Gap, Band Width, Promo Frequency, New SKU Detection — all derived from existing scrape data, require no new tables
